column5: Notes

column2: Continuous page scroll (all pages stacked browser-style)
column5: Re-rated Medium→Hard (2026-06): the single-page assumption is load-bearing everywhere — _screen_to_pdf would become screen→(page,x,y), every gesture/zoom/search/anchor path assumes one current page, and 'current page' drives the notes panel (scroll would constantly commit/restore notes while typing). Several sessions, high regression risk. Prerequisite: page→surface LRU render cache (shared with #23 thumbnails). Cheap alternative shipped as #20.

column2: Text selection (Alt+drag copies text like a browser)
column5: Alt+drag highlights words (word-level via page.get_text('words')); Ctrl+C copies in reading order with line/paragraph breaks. Alt+click opens PDF links. Status was stale — shipped in May.
Needs ocrmypdf installed; requires progress indicator for large files. Unchanged.
Live Typora-style rendering implemented with GtkSource TextTags; Ctrl+B/I/E formatting shortcuts added.
Errors show in Adw.AlertDialog with a Copy Error button (fixed GTK4 clipboard API).

column2: MX Master thumb button (btn 277) toggles pan mode
column5: Hold-to-pan (2026-06): hold button 10 and move the mouse to pan, release to stop (was a press-toggle via GestureSingle). Detected with EventControllerLegacy — the only layer that reports button-10 press AND release through pointer movement, since gestures cancel the sequence once a drag claims it (extras/probe_thumb.py). PyGObject quirk: the signal's event arg marshals as None, so the handler falls back to controller.get_current_event().
Added alongside thumb button work.

column2: Set as default application for PDFs
column5: .desktop file registered via xdg-mime; also registered for text/markdown.

column2: Auto-convert PPTX to PDF on open
column5: LibreOffice runs in background thread; shows Converting toast.
Migrated from Poppler to PyMuPDF; strokes saved as PDF ink annotations — individually erasable across sessions.
Ctrl+Shift+N inserts blank page; Ctrl+Shift+Del removes current page.
Intercepts close-request and open-file when dirty; Save / Discard / Cancel.
Requires matplotlib mathtext → Cairo pixmap → TextBuffer child anchor; manageable but needs TextMark bookkeeping for cursor-show/hide like Typora.
PyMuPDF: doc.copy_page / doc.move_page. Drag-to-reorder would need a separate page-thumbnail sidebar widget.

column2: Anchor markers linking notes to PDF spots
column5: Ctrl+Alt+click inserts <!-- anchor:X:Y --> at notes cursor; numbered circles drawn on canvas. Stored invisibly in the sidecar .md file. Cursor line in notes highlights the corresponding marker on the PDF; clicking a marker jumps the notes cursor to its line.

SearchEntry in a slide-down Revealer above the canvas. Searches all pages via fitz.search_for; highlights all hits in yellow, active match in orange. Next/prev with Enter/↑/↓ buttons; wraps around; navigates across pages automatically.

Superscripts (x^2 or x^{ab}), subscripts (x_1 or x_{ab}), and symbol shortcuts (\sum → Σ, \alpha → α, 30+ symbols). Markers hidden on non-cursor lines; symbols substituted in buffer and restored when cursor enters. rise+scale TextTags, no matplotlib.

Ctrl+E exports the open PDF with a notes page after each source page. Anchor markers drawn on source pages (numbered circles); notes text rendered inline with [N] markers replacing anchors. Option to include/skip empty-notes pages.
Operation-based undo stack: Ctrl+Z undoes draws and erase drags (one drag = one undo group); also restores strokes loaded from file. Stack re-keyed on page insert/delete.

column2: Scroll past page boundary flips page
column5: Scrolling 3 notches past a visible page edge flips the page (resistance resets on direction reversal). Fitted views re-fit the new page; zoomed views keep zoom/horizontal position and align the new page top (bottom when flipping back). Canvas-initiated page changes now fire on_page_will_change so the window commits notes first — also fixes note loss on link jumps and cross-page undo.

Snapshot dirty PDF+notes to ~/.local/state/sidemark/autosave/<path-hash>/ every 60s via canvas.save_copy(); original never touched until explicit save. On open: Recover / Discard / Not now dialog when a snapshot is newer than the file. Snapshot deleted on save and on discard-changes; pruned after 30 days at startup. Limitation: notes-only mode (.md without PDF) is not autosaved yet.

column2: Callouts: anchor note text rendered on the PDF with an arrow
column5: <!-- callout:X:Y --> marker paired with an anchor (working name was 'ship'). Ctrl+Alt+drag places anchor at press and callout box at release (dashed preview line during drag); plain Ctrl+Alt+click stays anchor-only. Paragraph text rendered in a wrapped box on canvas (PangoCairo, screen-space for crisp text) and in Ctrl+E export (height measured on a scratch page — insert_textbox silently renders nothing on overflow). Shared _parse_anchors() feeds canvas, window highlight logic and export. Drag-to-reposition still open.

doc.get_toc() → ListBox in a slide-out Revealer left of the split pane; rows indented by level, click navigates, Ctrl+T or header button toggles. Button disabled for PDFs without a TOC. Pairs with future page-thumbnail sidebar (#14).

Shipped: recent.json in ~/.local/share/sidemark (newest first, cap 15, scratchpad/blanks excluded); header MenuButton rebuilt on popover show; Gtk.RecentManager feeds recently-used.xbel (GTK dialogs, KF6 KRecentDocument/krunner). Walker/Omarchy: extras/sidemark_recent.lua elephant menu (jq-based, modeled on the local vscode.lua), installed via opt-in ./install.sh --walker-menu (xbel + default-app handling already covers most workflows); sidemark --list-recent prints name\tpath without importing GTK for other launchers. Skipped: dedicated krunner C++/KPackage plugin (xbel already covers KDE; a native runner would be ~1-2 days incl. packaging). Startup view instead of scratchpad still open.

Shipped: stroke dict gained 'opacity'; Pen/Highlighter segmented switcher at the top of the pen-settings popover (Ctrl+H flips it); while the highlighter is active the header pen button shows a live preview of the highlight stroke instead of the pencil icon. Pen-settings popover (width scale, color button, swatches) routes to whichever tool is active; highlighter defaults to yellow/12pt/0.40. Round-trips through the PDF CA key via annot.set_opacity / annot.opacity (fitz reports -1 when unset). Eraser, undo/redo and export work unchanged since strokes stay ordinary ink annotations.
Gtk.GestureStylus gives pressure, but PDF ink annotations have one width per annotation — variable-width strokes need segmenting into multiple annots. Needs hardware to verify.
Manifest + PyMuPDF module build is the main effort; Flathub would reach non-Arch users. No code changes.

column2: Keep PDF view adjusted when notes sidebar toggles or window resizes
column5: Canvas reacts to its GTK resize signal: while in fitted state (tracked via _is_fitted, cleared by any manual zoom/pan) a resize re-fits the page; a manually zoomed view keeps the PDF point at the viewport center anchored. Zoom level is never changed for zoomed views.

Single Ctrl+Z timeline across canvas and notes (fixes draw→type→draw undoing in the wrong order when focus stayed in the sidebar). Window keeps _undo_timeline: one entry per draw/erase gesture (canvas.on_user_action) and one per typing burst between two canvas actions (token stores the page and the text before the burst). Capture-phase key controller intercepts Ctrl+Z before the TextView (skipped when a Gtk.Editable like the search entry has focus). Notes undo restores the snapshot (irreversible set_text — buffer-native undo replaced by the timeline) and jumps to the token's page. Timeline re-keyed on page insert/delete, cleared on file open. Redo added as #31.

The notes panel sits in a Gtk.Paned (resizable), so the outline's Gtk.Revealer slide can't be reused — instead Adw.TimedAnimation drives paned set_position over 250ms (ease-out-cubic). Box hidden only when the collapse finishes; a 600ms skip-guard finishes the slide when no frames tick (offscreen tests, hidden window).

Mirrors the global undo timeline: canvas keeps a _redo_stack of op-lists exactly as undo_last popped them (erase groups round-trip); window keeps _redo_timeline with notes tokens storing before+after text. Any new user action (gesture or typing burst) clears redo. Same capture-phase key controller, header button next to undo. Both stacks re-keyed on page insert/delete, cleared on file open.

column2: Page-thumbnail sidebar when the PDF has no outline
column5: Shipped (2026-06): when get_toc() is empty, Ctrl+T fills the existing _toc_list with 96px Gdk.MemoryTexture thumbnails (numbered, SINGLE selection) rendered lazily via a GLib.idle_add queue invalidated on document change (~0.5ms/page text, 5-30ms scans, ~110KB each). Row click jumps; page changes select + center-scroll the current thumbnail; page insert/delete repopulates. Toggle now bounces only with no document open. Follow-up shipped: Outline ⇄ Pages switcher atop the sidebar when a TOC exists, and the sidebar narrows to hug the thumbnails in Pages view (230px for outline text). Strokes drawn after opening are not yet reflected in the thumbnails — live refresh plus page-reorder drag (#14) can build on a shared render cache later (also the prerequisite cache for #1 continuous scroll).

column2: Thumb button + scroll wheel zooms instead of panning
column5: Shipped (2026-06): _on_scroll routes to the cursor-anchored zoom path while _thumb_panning is latched; the thumb pan origin is rebased after each zoom step so the next motion event doesn't jump. Now hold-based together with #6 — hold the thumb button and scroll to zoom, release to stop (was a press-toggle), via the EventControllerLegacy press/release detection documented in #6.
Shipped (2026-06): btn==2 branch in _on_drag_begin sets the same _panning state as the Ctrl+drag path; drag-update/end pan handling needed no changes.

Shipped (2026-06): PageUp/PageDown and mouse buttons 8/9 route through window._nav_page → canvas._flip_page, the same path as #20 scroll-past-edge flips: fitted views re-fit, zoomed views keep zoom and align the new page's top (bottom when going back). Bounds-clamped no-op at first/last page.

column2: Install opt-in: register as default handler for PowerPoint files
column5: Shipped (2026-06): ./install.sh --register-pptx runs xdg-mime default for both PowerPoint mime types (the .desktop file already declared them). Off by default — most users want an office suite as their .pptx handler.

column2: Hold after drawing snaps stroke to straight line (GoodNotes-style)
column5: On motion-stop (GLib.timeout_add ~500 ms) activate straight-line mode: replace the in-progress polyline preview with a single line from start-point to cursor, track cursor until release, commit as a normal ink annotation. Needs a drawing state machine (free → holding → straight) and a canvas preview redraw during the hold phase without committing. No annotation format changes.

Gtk.GestureZoom gives a scale factor delta and handles both touchscreen pinch and touchpad pinch (GDK touchpad gesture events) in GTK4. On begin: record viewport anchor at pinch centroid (get_bounding_box_center); on scale-changed: apply zoom centred on that anchor — same math as the existing Ctrl+scroll zoom. Main risk: interplay with the EventControllerScroll (two-finger touchpad scroll = pan) on the same canvas; needs claim/deny tuning. Cannot test touchscreen without hardware; touchpad pinch testable locally.

column2: Drag-and-drop a file onto the window to open it
column5: Gtk.DropTarget accepting text/uri-list on the ApplicationWindow. On drop: parse the URI, check extension (.pdf/.pptx/.md), call the existing open-file path. Very additive — no interaction with drawing or notes.

column2: Header bar should stay visible in fullscreen
column5: Root cause found (2026-06): the header is attached via set_titlebar(), and GTK4 hides the titlebar area in fullscreen by design — not a Hyprland issue. Fix: restructure so the header is a normal child (Gtk.Box with header on top, or Adw.ToolbarView) instead of a titlebar. Small but structural: window layout, CSS (flat headerbar styling differs outside the titlebar slot), and the GTK-drawn window controls disappear — needs a quick visual check. Re-rated Easy→Easy-Medium.

column2: Toggle between draw mode and text-select mode
column5: Verified (2026-06): the default (modifier-less) drag branch in _on_drag_begin starts drawing; select mode just routes it to the existing Alt+drag text-selection state instead — selection logic ships already (#2). Add a canvas mode flag + header toggle button (mirror the pen/highlighter switcher #25) + shortcut; Alt+drag stays as the always-available path. Needs a clear mode indicator (header icon and/or text cursor on canvas). Right-drag erase should probably stay active in select mode — decide during impl.
